title: cirsium acaule subsp. gregarium
intro: Cirsium acaule subsp. gregarium, also known as the stemless thistle or dwarf thistle, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and leafy rosettes. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The herbal preparations of Cirsium acaule subsp. gregarium include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which have been used to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support digestive health.
